name: Monstera obliqua 'Zamora-Chinchipe Form'
aboutText: The Monstera obliqua 'Zamora-Chinchipe' is a highly sought-after locality form of the iconic Swiss cheese plant, originating from the montane cloud forests of Ecuador. Unlike the more common broad-leaf varieties, this form is distinguished by its refined, delicate lamina and restrained, narrow fenestrations. As a hemiepiphytic climber, it thrives in cool, perpetually misted environments, utilizing its aerial roots to ascend mossy substrates. Its aesthetic is defined by slender, lanceolate leaves and a delicate, graceful growth habit that represents one of the most stable and collectible forms of this species.

fieldNotes.title: Zamora-Chinchipe's Humid Valleys
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: From Zamora-Chinchipe province in southeastern Ecuador — the wet montane forests of the Podocarpus National Park region, where the eastern Andes transition into the Amazonian lowlands through a series of humid ridges and river valleys. Ultra rare classification, but at accessible price tier, which is somewhat unexpected. Zamora-Chinchipe is genuinely botanically significant — it was the source of numerous Anthurium and Monstera collections by Croat and others in the 1980s-2000s. Authentic material from documented Zamoran obliqua populations would be a meaningful acquisition.
